working_definition: Type 1 conventional dendritic cell (cDC1) in adult human skin. Identified in Reynolds et al. (2021) as the DC1 population within the mononuclear phagocyte landscape. Defined by CLEC9A, XCR1, and CD141 (BDCA-3) expression. Specialised for cross-presentation of antigens on MHC class I to CD8+ T cells. Adult population; fetal DC1 is documented separately.

best_match.cl_definition: A conventional dendritic cell that expresses XCR1 and CLEC9A and is specialised for cross-presentation of antigens on MHC class I. In human, also known as BDCA-3+/CD141+ cDC.

justification: CL:0002399 'conventional dendritic cell type 1' directly corresponds to the DC1 population in Reynolds et al. (2021), which is confirmed by enrichment of XCR1+DC gene signatures and expression of CLEC9A, XCR1, and CD141.
recommendation: Use CL:0002399 (conventional dendritic cell type 1) as an exact match for the DC1 cell type.
